summary: Descriptive overview of adversepossession in substantive law: elements, statutory periods, policy rationales.
key_points: elements is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for adversepossession are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine adversepossession in litigation.
The doctrine of adversepossession has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding adversepossession requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
